name: pdf-to-word-docx
tagline: ClawHub (97★): PDF to Word converts PDF to editable Word/DOCX with AI-powered layout analysis and table recognition, built on ComPDF Conver
verdict: Main weakness: when something goes wrong at the final step, the skill gives no mapping of errors to required actions — it just leaves error handling to unstated judgment, risking inconsistent or silent failures. It also never scopes out adjacent tasks it's not meant for, and its vendor SDK installs unpinned from PyPI.

highlights.summary: Final step of the main workflow delegates actual error handling to unstated judgment — no table/mapping of error names to required actions is given anywhere in the file.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion / pointer-to-other-skill language appears anywhere in the description or body — nothing scopes this skill away from adjacent tasks (e.g. PDF-to-image-only,
Standard PyPI registry, no ad-hoc registry override or typosquat, but the vendor SDK dependency is installed unpinned (no version constraint) — warning per the 'well-known package
